# pebble

> pebble is an independent research lab investigating matrix-valued token representations, byte-level inputs, and measurable reasoning in language models. Findings are published as falsifiable research notes with code, eval data, and references. The research log is maintained by an autonomous agent under Sam Larson's supervision; all claims are verified against experiments run on real hardware.

## Findings

- [The operator bank: architecture proven, exact contender convergence-recovered, far depth still open](https://pebbleml.com/findings/ncr-operator-bank.html): Extending Native Composition Reads (single-relation exact O(log h) composition) to a bank of R=3 relations. The exact contender would not converge at R=3 even at 3x the proven single-relation training budget. The bank architecture itself is proven regardless, by a fully-trained fast-weight-memory baseline: in-distribution recovered@0.9 = 0.87-0.89 across 3 relations, relation-swap ablation gap 0.621 (genuine relation-selective capability isolation). A targeted diagnosis recovered the exact contender's convergence: annealing an inter-hop LayerNorm out during training unsticks it where three other fixes reproduce flat-chance failure -- in-distribution recovered@0.9 goes 0.0 to 1.0/1.0/1.0. A seed replication then ran to n=9 total (the original seed plus 8 more): convergence held 9/9 (every seed 1.000 in-distribution, 0/9 dead seeds). Far depth is SEED-BIMODAL, not uniformly open: at h*=61 the 9 seeds' min-over-r recovered@0.9 sorted reads 0.000/0.000/0.000/0.004/0.012/0.615/0.811/0.918/0.984 -- only 2/9 clear the strict 0.9 threshold, with an observed (not causal) pairing between lower converged phase residual and higher far-depth recovery. UPDATE (2026-07-12): a pre-registered 16-cell follow-up tested whether the same earlyln fix rescues the separate single-relation K-scaling wall (K=14/15/16/24, 4 seeds each, worst-of-scored pooled verdict). It does not unlock the K axis wholesale -- pooled verdict TRAINABILITY-STILL-LIMITED -- but it moves the wall exactly one rung: K=15, plain-recipe DEAD at n=1, is now 4/4 CONVERGED and HOLDS far-depth composition (median recovered@0.9 = 0.9929 at h*=117); K=16 converges in only 1/4 seeds and fails far depth at the first ladder rung; K=24 fails outright (0/4) but with a distinct partial-formation profile (loss descends, rank climbs to ~17.7/24) pointing at budget/anneal-schedule as the next lever. Bonus finding: earlyln also improves write quality at every rung it converges (K=14's residual drops 0.0072 to 0.0020-0.0042). FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-12): two priced probes then ran. A 2x budget/anneal probe (8 cells) moved K=16's Gate-1 convergence 1/4 to 3/4 and its failure front 13 to 29 in 3/4 seeds, but far depth stayed exactly 0.0; K=24 was budget-flat-to-worse (mean residual rose, one seed collapsed further, indist stayed 0/4). A pre-registered next-lever design then set up a three-story discriminator (Story S1: tight state-spare is worse; Story S2: the wall tracks the conventional d=2K state-sizing rule, not absolute K; the registry's own absolute-K-cliff reading) and ran 20 cells (~13.61 GPU-h): a 4x-budget probe at K=16 read NO-LAW, not a slowing law -- write residual got WORSE from 2x to 4x in 3 of 4 seeds and one 2x-converged seed's failure front regressed 29 to 13 at 4x, tripping two of the design's three independent pre-registered anomaly triggers. Retraining at the tight-spare d=K+1 mapping instead of the conventional d=2K CONFIRMED at both K=16 and K=24: 4/4 CONVERGED at both K on 1x budget alone (versus 1/4 at K=16/d=32 even after 4x budget across 16 K=16/d=32 cells at 1x/2x/4x/anneal-0.75, and 0/4 at K=24/d=48 across all 12 K=24/d=48 cells tried at 1x/2x/anneal-0.75 -- no 4x variant was ever run at K=24) -- falsifying Story S1 and the absolute-K-cliff story at both K simultaneously and confirming Story S2 (the converged K=16/d=17 write residual, 0.0028-0.0058, lands in S2's predicted 0.002-0.005 band, 60-125x below S1's predicted ~0.35). K=16 under tight-spare also reached real far-depth composition (0.80-0.99 recovered@0.9 at h*=125 in 2/4 seeds) that no d=2K cell reached at any budget or anneal-shape tried; K=24 under tight-spare solved Gate-1 but its far depth stayed weak and seed-variable (fronts 21-189, sweep-minimum recovery <=0.051 in all 4). A parallel anneal-shape probe (anneal_frac 0.75) partially confirmed at K=16 (Gate-1 1/4 to 2/4, residual down 38.5%, zero far-depth movement) and falsified at K=24 (indistinguishable from baseline). NOT established (at the time): the shape of the correct d(K) mapping law within K<=24, the mechanism behind d=2K's failure, and K=24's far-depth reliability even under tight-spare (fronts spanning trivial to h* within one 4-seed cohort). GENERALIZATION TEST (2026-07-12): a follow-up 12-cell K=32 grid (d in {K+1, 1.25K, 1.5K} x 4 seeds, queue jobs 009-020, ~6.37 GPU-h) answered the K>24 question directly, and the answer is NO: every cell dead at every d tried, including Gate-1 in-distribution convergence itself (0/12 CONVERGED; best cell 0.871 recovered@0.9, under the 0.9 bar; write-residual range 0.40-1.31; failure front pinned at 29, =K-3, the trivial train-residue rung, in all 12 cells; far-depth recovery 0.0 everywhere at h*=253). This is a stronger negative than "converges but doesn't compose far" -- basic Gate-1 convergence itself fails at K=32 even at the exact d=K+1 mapping that reached 4/4 CONVERGED at both K=16 and K=24. Verdict: CONVERGENCE REPLICATED 9/9 (bank), FAR DEPTH SEED-BIMODAL (bank); TRAINABILITY-STILL-LIMITED, WALL MOVES ONE RUNG under the conventional d=2K mapping, then THE WALL IS SUBSTANTIALLY A DIMENSION-MAPPING-CONVENTION ARTIFACT AT K<=24 (NOT an absolute-K or compute ceiling within that range) per the pre-registered three-story discriminator, BUT a K-dependent component of the wall re-emerges beyond K=24 -- the K<=24 finding and the d=2K leakage mechanism finding both stand; only the "not an absolute-K limit" claim is scoped to K<=24 (K-scaling). K-AXIS CLOSED (2026-07-12): a final pre-registered probe (NCR_MAPPING_LAW_DESIGN.md Sec 4) asked whether extra training budget rescues K=32/d=33 -- the "least dead" cell class in the generalization grid -- into robust convergence. 8 cells (budgets 2x=160,000 steps and 4x=320,000 steps, 4 seeds each, ~12.08 GPU-h) found: Gate-1 convergence improves directionally (0/4 at 1x, 1/4 at 2x, 2/4 at 4x) but never reaches the 3/4 CONVERGED-ROBUST bar at any budget, and the failure front is pinned at the trivial K-3 rung (=29) in all 12 cells now on record for this arm across all three budgets, with rec@h*(h=253) reading 0.0000 in every one. The probe's own pre-registered ANOMALY check fired (write residual dips then rises non-monotonically across 1x-to-2x-to-4x in 3 of 4 seeds, the same signature as the K16 4x precedent) which by the design's own binding exhaustiveness clause pre-empts the mechanical four-way verdict map entirely -- no (a)/(b1)/(b2)/(c) label is assigned. Stated plainly: this is the pre-registration working as designed, not a dodge. The practical question resolves independent of the anomaly, however: the sole outcome that could re-open the K-ladder (unblocking K=48's own d(K) grid) requires BOTH >=3/4 CONVERGED at some budget AND that budget's median failure front >= h*=253; neither half is met by the raw numbers at any budget tested, so K=48's reserved grid and the rest of the parked K>=48 backlog (~144 GPU-h) stay blocked regardless. THIS CLOSES THE K-AXIS BOOK AT K=32. Not established: why K=32 fails at every d tried including d=K+1 -- the Q3 leakage-shape mechanism (normalized leakage 7-14x larger at d=2K vs d=K+1) explains the K<=24 regime but was explicitly scoped not to speak to K=32's failure at every d; whether a larger budget (8x+) or a different lever would rescue it is untested and not licensed by this record. A genuine positive fell out of the same wave: seed-extending K=24's tight-spare (d=25) cells from the original n=4 to n=12 finds far-depth reliability is DECISIVELY PREDICTABLE from each seed's own converged write residual -- Spearman rho(delta, failure front) = -0.877 and rho(delta, sweep_min_recovered) = -0.873 (both n=12), far past the pre-registered |rho|>=0.6 bar, both negative as predicted (lower write residual -> further and more reliable far-depth recovery). This is a free, validated instrument: a number already produced at ordinary convergence time predicts far-depth composition without running the expensive depth sweep -- though it does not make K=24/d=25's far depth reliable in absolute terms (0/12 seeds clear the strict 0.9 HOLD bar under the whole-sweep-window metric, vs 4/12 under the looser single-point front>=h* metric). Total across the whole finding: ~58.41 GPU-h across eleven sub-experiments. Unpublished.
- [The null that survives is the one you can trust](https://pebbleml.com/findings/reasoning-link-instrument-saga.html): A gauntlet-required positive control on the production readout behind a published "80/80 nulls at every scale" result FAILED: a synthetic K=32 single-cycle recovered 0/256 through the production scoring path on a real checkpoint while the identical code recovered 1.0000 transposed — the third independent victim of this codebase's own [K,V]-vs-[V,K] state-layout transpose-bug class (closed-form adjudication confirmed exactly). A one-line fix, a kill-proofed regression test, and an independent audit later, re-running all 80 committed grid cells overturned the published null: 78/320 (cell,hop) readings turned nonzero, up to 0.87 recovery at one hop — the lane provisionally reopened. Two correspondence nulls pre-registered before any GPU pass (a label-shuffle null, then a positional derangement-slot null added after the first was found vacuous at one hop by construction) both reproduce the ENTIRE signal at 0/320 clears; strongest cell 0.8691 real vs 0.8125 deranged (93.5% reproduction vs the 50% trivial-artifact bar). Mechanism: cross-query prediction convergence 0.9996 against a near-collinear value population (mean pairwise cosine 0.9648) clears any threshold regardless of target. Lane re-closes, now doubly instrument-validated; corrected claim is "recovery is null-indistinguishable," never "recovery reads zero." Methodology finding; no capability claim. Unpublished.
- [O(log h) reads: 20.9x faster at depth 1,021, and it composes exactly](https://pebbleml.com/findings/native-composition-reads.html): Native Composition Reads (NCR) writes a relation operator in context and reads its h-fold composition EXACTLY at query time via scale-managed binary exponentiation (O(log h) sequential cost), against O(h) sequential baselines (fast-weight-memory recursive reads, a param-matched iterated-vector-map baseline) that must apply their step h times. CROSS-K VERDICT OF RECORD (wave-1 K=8 + Phase-2 K=12 replication, later extended to n=10 seeds at K=12): AXIS A WIN-PARTIAL — K=8 WIN (median recovered_frac@0.9 = 1.0 at h*=61, 5/5 seeds hold, vs 0.158 best baseline and 0.0 param-matched vector baseline; uniform across all novel residue strata, zero mod-reducer confounds; per-seed failure fronts median 253, inside the pre-registered [87,442] band) plus K=12 SEP-PARTIAL (pooled 10-seed median 0.8704 at h*=57, DEGRADED band, 5/10 seeds hold, vs 0.270/0.0 baselines, both FAIL band; uniform DEGRADED across all eight novel residue strata) — per the pre-registered cross-K table, WIN + SEP-PARTIAL = WIN-PARTIAL, the claim ceiling, confirmed unchanged at n=10, not negotiated after the readout. The K=12 asymmetric-confidence prediction (P1-K12) scored 2/3 legs: every predicted-hold and predicted-fail seed called correctly, but the directional "single-mode bound" leg was refuted — the conservative all-modes bound alone called all 5 seeds' hold/fail outcome exactly, a genuine calibration finding. AXIS B (read cost) WIN — 20.9x faster than the best O(h) arm at h=1,021 (bar 10x, K=8 label of record); every O(h) arm scales linear-in-h at R^2>=0.998 while the O(log h) read stays flat, a ~13,000-25,000x gap at h~2^20 (2.6ms vs 34.7-66.1s); K=12 supplementary timing (33.7-63.8x at h=1,533, no bar moved) replicates the same separation. AXIS C (calibration-locked trust curves) TIE at both K — 4/5 seeds track within 0.05 through h<=125, diverge by h=509 (disclosed instrument limitation). Depth is input-supplied to every arm; learned depth selection was killed analytically at design review, never empirically tried. The param-matched vector baseline cannot fit the task even in-distribution (~0.002 at K=8) and is a disclosed strawman risk; fast-weight memory is the load-bearing baseline. 42.3 GPU-h serial-sum (wave-1 18.38 + Phase-2 21.12 + K=12 seed extension 2.07) vs the 120 GPU-h program cap. An extension to a bank of R=3 relations is a separate finding, see "The operator bank" above. Unpublished.
- [Composes exactly at 8x training depth, where it converges](https://pebbleml.com/findings/composes-where-it-trains.html): The diagnosis closing broken-lens.html's pending promotion call, run entirely from committed checkpoints (152/152 harness-fidelity teeth). Every miss in the Stage-2 compositional-depth endpoint is mechanism-diagnosed as a trainability event, not an instrument defect and not architecture insufficiency: A6's n_h<=2 configs hit a depth-graded architecture ceiling (effective rank never reaches d_min=5) while n_h=4 pins at rank 5.00 and reads crosscheck recovery 1.000 flat to 8x training depth (zero seed variance, 3/3 seeds) vs the restricted baseline at exactly 0.0; S5's one missing seed is a rank-deficient basin that satisfies training loss without the faithful representation (a worse-loss sibling is fully faithful); A5's 3/5 non-convergences are an optimization lottery with measured non-responsiveness to budget. Ratified verdict of record: INCONCLUSIVE-TRAINABILITY-LIMITED — exact composition at 8x training depth in every converged configuration, trainability qualifier mandatory wherever that claim appears. No capability win claimed. Unpublished.
- [When the instrument is the failure](https://pebbleml.com/findings/broken-lens.html): On a 62-cell compositional-depth sweep, the primary readout and its pre-registered crosscheck contradicted each other at 0-vs-1.0 magnitude on exactly the perfectly converged cells (both read ~0 on every non-converged cell). Tiebreak: the primary lens's identity-gauge assumption was validated on a different architecture and silently failed on this one; the corrected read survived a shuffled-target falsifier pinned in advance (0.00/0.00/0.05, all far below the 0.5 void bar). Corrected endpoint INCONCLUSIVE — one gating group shows full far-depth separation (crosscheck 1.000 vs baseline 0.0), the other misses on a single trainability-outlier seed. Methodology finding; no capability win claimed. UPDATED: the same [K,V]/[V,K] transpose-bug class independently recurred a third time in an unrelated program (finding no. 15). Unpublished.
- [Constant-memory recall](https://pebbleml.com/findings/constant-memory-recall.html): A 14M fast-weight model holds K=32 episodic recall at acc_A 0.998-1.000 (every seed, every horizon) with a recurrent state fixed at 32,768 bytes, out to 1,798-token contexts (8x the binding window). The param/token-matched transformer reads chance at every horizon even with an UNCAPPED KV cache, and capping at M in {1..32} slots changes nothing — so the pre-registered degenerate-baseline clause fired and NO memory-multiplier number is claimed; verdict of record: "baseline non-competitive at matched params/tokens." Episode-restricted argmax recall (Nichani caveat); not a capacity claim. Unpublished.
- [The rank law](https://pebbleml.com/findings/rank-law.html): Across five finite groups with provable minimum representation dimensions, a fast-weight model's recruited effective rank tracks d_min (Spearman rho 0.9747, tie-capped maximum), the S4-vs-A5 marquee pair lands on dimension rather than solvability, and a forced-rank causal test steps from exactly zero recovery at k=d_min-1 to anchor-level recovery at k=d_min in all five groups. Preprint in preparation.
- [Fast-weight recall separation](https://pebbleml.com/findings/fast-weight-recall.html): At 14M parameters on K=32 associative recall, a fast-weight matrix-state model reads 0.999 recall vs 0.045 (param-matched flat-vector ablation) and 0.030 (FLOP/data-matched transformer; chance 0.031). Zeroing the block-0 state collapses recall to chance; the binding is causally in S0 but only linearly decodable after downstream nonlinear processing. CONFIRMED at n=3 fresh seeds: paired CIs on both gaps exclude the pre-registered 0.30 margin. Unpublished.
- [The write-geometry attractor](https://pebbleml.com/findings/write-geometry-attractor.html): Key-write geometry in DeltaNet-family LMs collapses monotonically more with scale (span_frac 0.248 at 14M to 0.455 at 1.31B). A pre-registered 2x2 exonerates qk-l2-norm as the cause (0.05-sigma null at n=3); decay gating shows a direction-consistent worsening trend NOT confirmed at the pre-registered 2-sigma bar. UPDATED with the fix-at-scale wave verdict: NO tested frozen-bias construction stabilizes the attractor at scale — the deployed per_token arm stays destabilizing (CI-excludes-zero at 98M both corpora, 392M-wikitext), the 14M global-vector stabilization decays to ~zero and sign-flips by 392M; val-loss neutrality is the half that transfers (8/8 gates). Preprint in preparation.
- [Super-linear capacity](https://pebbleml.com/findings/superlinear-capacity.html): The capacity ratio at which a trained DeltaNet fast-weight state's held-out recall collapses rises with state dimension — logistic midpoint x0 0.5455 at d=64 vs 0.6779 at d=80, CI-excluding ratio-invariance; no cliff measurable at d=96 through K/d=0.94 (near-ceiling fine structure instrument-limited). Preprint in preparation.
- [The gradient does not see rank](https://pebbleml.com/findings/matrix-codi-rank-blindness.html): A structural explanation for the Illusion of Superposition in latent chain-of-thought. Four flat rank-k curves across training conditions; three-seed accuracy-rank decoupling; four positive-control readouts nonlinear in Z all fail to bend the rank-k curve. Published at the ICML 2026 Mechanistic Interpretability Workshop; paper PDF at https://pebbleml.com/assets/papers/the-gradient-does-not-see-rank.pdf
- [Matrix operations are 128× more parameter-efficient per layer](https://pebbleml.com/findings/parameter-efficiency.html): At d=16, a matrix transformer layer uses 128× fewer parameters than the flattened d²×d² linear equivalent -- a per-layer parameter-count claim, not a matched-capacity or matched-compute claim.
- [Output mechanism shapes rank trajectory in matrix-valued refinement](https://pebbleml.com/findings/output-head-dynamics.html): Bilinear probes see rank rise across 8 iterations; vector-collapse and 3D matrix-product runs see rank fall. Observational, single-seed.
- [Rank enrichment is an emergent, novel phenomenon](https://pebbleml.com/findings/rank-enrichment.html): With a bilinear output head, the effective rank of matrix token representations rises during iterative refinement (5.02 → 6.12), counter to the prior literature's depth-collapse assumption.
- [Outer-product matrix embeddings outperform flat vectors per parameter](https://pebbleml.com/findings/outer-product-embedding.html): At single-step processing, a matrix token from outer-product embedding beats a flat-vector baseline by 26% lower BPB at 2.2x fewer parameters (Run 22, param-matched ablation), and by 175x in perplexity on a larger reasoning corpus (Run 12 vs Run 13).

## Context

- [A reading of the field](https://pebbleml.com/findings/literature-synthesis.html): Multi-agent literature synthesis across continuous reasoning, JEPA, structured representations, byte-level models, and the neuroscience of language.

## About

- [Pebble homepage](https://pebbleml.com): Research thesis, current experiments, roadmap, and dead ends.
- Source repository: https://github.com/saml212/learned-representations
- Contact: sam@pebbleml.com
